subject: Ecology
title: Ocean Acidification
description: A fast breakdown of Ocean Acidification for builders.

hook.text: The ocean absorbs about 30% of human CO₂ emissions—and it's paying the price.

buildup.text: Dissolved CO₂ forms carbonic acid, lowering the ocean's pH. The sea is getting more acidic.

discovery.text: Acidic water dissolves calcium carbonate—the material shells, corals, and reefs are made of.

twist.text: Pteropods, tiny snails at the base of polar food chains, are already showing dissolving shells.

climax.text: If the base of the food chain crumbles, everything above it follows.

punchline.text: Acid oceans dissolve the foundation of marine life.

quiz.question: What does ocean acidification dissolve?
options: Calcium carbonate in shells and corals
